results.title: Turkish police force ousted opposition out of headquarters as crisis deepens
results.description: ANKARA — Turkish riot police fired tear gas and forced their way into the main opposition party’s headquarters to evict its ousted leadership on Sunday, fuelling a crisis at the heart of Turkey’s democracy. The post Turkish police force ousted opposition out of headquarters as crisis deepens appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .

results.title: With big decisions ahead, the Supreme Court collides with a testy Trump
results.description: WASHINGTON — Vice President JD Vance made an unannounced visit to the Supreme Court this month to attend a private dinner in a wood-paneled conference room with Chief Justice John Roberts and dozens of the chief justice’s former law clerks. The post With big decisions ahead, the Supreme Court collides with a testy Trump appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .

results.title: Russia hits Ukraine with Oreshnik missile in one of war’s biggest attacks on Kyiv
results.description: KYIV — Russia pounded Kyiv and surrounding areas with hundreds of drones and missiles on Sunday in one of the heaviest bombardments of the city since the start of the four-year war, firing an Oreshnik hypersonic missile near the capital. The post Russia hits Ukraine with Oreshnik missile in one of war’s biggest attacks on Kyiv appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .

results.title: Letter: Keauhou Bay bungalow plan raises questions
results.description: On June 15 at 2 p.m., at the courthouse in Kailua-Kona, a motion-to- dismiss hearing will be held involving Kamehameha Schools’ proposed Keauhou Bay bungalow resort development and the challenged final environmental impact statement.

results.title: Letter: Mayor should select HPD chief after 3 failures
results.description: Regarding the new HPD chief, in recent memory the Honolulu Police Commission has selected Louis Kealoha, Susan Ballard and Arthur “Joe” Logan, while — sometimes repeatedly — rejecting Mike Lambert, Ben Moszkowicz and Rade Vanic. The selectees share the common attribute of ultimately failing in the job, while the rejects share the not-so-common attribute of succeeding when they served as head of a local police or law enforcement department.

results.title: The Navy’s inexcusable accommodation of Kash Patel
results.description: The USS Arizona is a tomb. That should have been enough. The post The Navy’s inexcusable accommodation of Kash Patel appeared first on Hawaii Tribune-Herald .
